content: AI agent workflows can be dynamically modularized around working memory capacity limits, rather than being built as fixed linear or branching task sequences: when approaching a complex open-ended goal, the workflow splits long reasoning chains into independent memory-aligned chunks that are stitched together only when needed, rather than forcing the agent to hold all intermediate context in its limited working memory. This creates a new 'capacity-aware agent workflow' design paradigm that adapts task structure to the agent's inherent cognitive bounds, rather than expecting the agent to adapt to a pre-built workflow.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
